snippets.pull_quote: Shifting gears back and forth between client work and business development is a little bit tricky to manage. I find it hard to just constantly be going back and forth between those two zones.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to the mental cost of toggling between delivery and BD — fractionals feel this daily.

snippets.pull_quote: It's pretty bumpy. I can go long periods without any new client inquiries, and then I'll get a couple at a time. I'd like a more steady state of new client opportunities.
snippets.website_angle: Pipeline unpredictability is the core anxiety — use this to frame consistent BD support.

snippets.pull_quote: It was very generic. It sounded like all the other posts on LinkedIn. So I've been exploring finding someone who won't use AI — really writing higher quality content.
snippets.website_angle: Position against commodity AI content; operators want voice, not volume.

snippets.pull_quote: My gut says that's going to come by posting good quality content on LinkedIn. It seems like that is the best way for me to market myself.
snippets.website_angle: Validate LinkedIn as the right bet for fractionals — then show how to do it without the grind.

snippets.pull_quote: The success rate with email campaigns is very low. You have to have a huge volume to just get one quality lead.
snippets.website_angle: Acknowledge what doesn't work for solo operators — builds credibility before pitching alternatives.

snippets.pull_quote: The typical fractional CFO is chasing SaaS and tech-focused businesses. I'm a little more old school — healthcare and manufacturing is where I have experience.
snippets.website_angle: Highlight that niche depth beats broad positioning; help operators articulate what makes them different.

snippets.pull_quote: I haven't lost a client yet, but I know it's going to happen eventually. That's why I'm concerned about having a certain pipeline — so I don't go back to zero.
snippets.website_angle: Tap the pre-emptive fear of churn; pipeline isn't optional, it's insurance.

snippets.pull_quote: I'm happy working 35, no more than 40 hours a week. I'm not looking to burn the midnight oil. The work-lifestyle equation really shifts if you start building a team.
snippets.website_angle: Fractionals chose this path for a reason — messaging should honor the lifestyle, not just the revenue.

snippets.pull_quote: Marketing work gets pushed aside and I end up focusing on a client need instead. That happens. And quarterly goals get stretched.
snippets.website_angle: Show how reactive client demands quietly kill proactive growth — a relatable friction point.

snippets.pull_quote: A successful week is one where I can devote time to both client work and business development without shuffling things around. The week goes as planned and I've moved project work forward.
snippets.website_angle: Define the dream outcome in operators' own terms — progress over firefighting.

snippets.pull_quote: That spreadsheet required constant manual coordination. That's a little bit what I'm looking to solve — automating connections between accounting software and reports.
snippets.website_angle: Surface the hidden time tax of manual financial workflows for fractional CFOs.
